slides.title: Deckaster
slides.subtitle: Turn any project into a self-explanatory deck and a narrated, word-highlighted video.
slides.narration: Meet Deckaster. It turns any project into a deck and a narrated video that explains itself.

slides.title: Great work goes unexplained because making a polished walkthrough is slow.
slides.body: Designers and builders ship fast, but turning that into a clear deck and video takes hours in slide and timeline editors.
slides.narration: Here's the problem. People build great things, but explaining them well is painfully slow.

slides.title: Built for designers and vibecoders who'd rather build than narrate.
slides.body: The people closest to the work are the worst-placed to spend an afternoon in a video editor.
slides.narration: It's for designers and vibecoders who would rather keep building than wrestle with editing tools.

slides.title: The deck already knows the script — so the video should make itself.
slides.body: If an agent can write the slides, it can write the narration, speak it, and sync the highlights automatically.
slides.narration: The key insight: if the agent can write the slides, it can also narrate them and sync the highlights.

slides.title: One skill, five stages, zero timeline editing.
slides.narration: Deckaster runs as a single skill with five stages, so you never touch a timeline editor.

slides.title: An HTML-first, deterministic pipeline.

visual.code:
flowchart LR
  A[Project] --> B[Extract style]
  B --> C[Generate deck]
  C --> D[Narrate + timestamps]
  D --> E[Render video]
  E --> F[final.mp4]
visual.alt: Pipeline from project to final video

slides.narration: Under the hood it's an HTML-first pipeline that renders deterministically, so results are reproducible.

slides.title: What makes it different.

cards.heading: On-brand
cards.text: Pulls colors and fonts from your project automatically.

cards.heading: Self-narrating
cards.text: Writes and speaks a script that complements the slides.

cards.heading: Karaoke sync
cards.text: Highlights each word on the slide as it is spoken.

slides.narration: Three things set it apart: it's on-brand, it narrates itself, and it highlights words as they're spoken.

slides.title: From idea to explainer in minutes.

stat.label: faster than hand-editing a narrated walkthrough

quote.text: It felt like the deck explained itself.

slides.narration: In testing it was about ten times faster than hand-editing a narrated walkthrough.

slides.title: Where it's going.
bullets: In-slide motion accents and auto-animate transitions
slides.narration: Next up: richer motion, more voices and languages, and one-click PowerPoint export.

slides.title: Point it at your project. Get back a deck and a video.
slides.body: No timeline. No editing. Just your work, explained.
slides.narration: Point Deckaster at your project, and get back a deck and a video. Give it a try.
